is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study concerns P. falciparum malaria treatment; blood films positive for P. falciparum in two children.

reports_target_markers.evidence: This is a pharmacokinetic study of quinine metabolism; no molecular drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfk13, etc.) are genotyped or reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: No molecular marker frequencies reported; only pharmacokinetic parameters.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from Hôpital Provincial, Port-Gentil, Gabon — a specific town/clinic below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Received 04 May 1995; study conducted around 1994-1995, a window of at most 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Fifteen Gabonese children treated for malaria at Port-Gentil, Gabon.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical pharmacokinetic study in enrolled children.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site clinical pharmacokinetic study with no mention of consortium or shared dataset.

supplement.evidence: No molecular data exists anywhere in this pharmacokinetic study; no supplement would provide target marker frequencies.

assessment.summary: A 1996 pharmacokinetic study comparing quinine metabolism in 15 malnourished vs normally-nourished Gabonese children with malaria. It reports pharmacokinetic parameters (clearance, half-life, AUC), not P. falciparum drug-resistance molecular markers or their frequencies. Fails the core marker/frequency criteria.
exclusion_reasons: No curated drug-resistance codon markers genotyped
No extractable per-locus mutation frequencies (pharmacokinetic study only)
